question: Will the total dollar amount of mortgage-backed securities held by the US Federal Reserve Banks have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-05-11?
resolution_criteria:
Resolves to the value found at https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/WSHOMCB once the data is published.

The exact value on 2025-05-11 is unknown, but the most recent known value (as of 2025-05-01) was 2185406.000. (The latest value released in Assets: Securities Held Outright: Mortgage-Backed Securities: Wednesday Level from the release H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances.)
background:
The notes from the release: For questions on the data, please contact the data source: https://www.federalreserve.gov/apps/ContactUs/feedback.aspx?refurl=/releases/h41/%
For questions on FRED functionality, please contact: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/contactus/.  The notes from the series: The current face value of mortgage-backed obligations held by Federal Reserve Banks. These securities are guaranteed by Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, or Ginnie Mae..  Additional background of the series:  1. the units of the series: Millions of U.S. Dollars.  2. the seasonal adjustments of the series:  Not Seasonally Adjusted  3. the update frequency: Weekly, As of Wednesday
